feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 100 to 130 mm of rain (possibly more in localised areas) mainly from Ruahine range northwards. Peak rates of 10 to 20 mm/h expected during the Thursday about the Hawke's Bay ranges. Note, heavy rain in northern Hawke's Bay may continue into Friday morning and the validity of this warning could be extended. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Watch upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: South to southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: High.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of rain which may be heavy at times. Snow level lowering to 900 metres by midnight tonight, to 800 metres tomorrow morning and to 500 metres tomorrow afternoon. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Red Warning downgraded to a Watch. Period extended to 6 pm Thursday.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 80 to 120 mm about the Tararua Range, and 40 to 80 mm about the eastern hills of Wellington and Wairarapa. Peak rainfall rates 10 to 20 mm per hour. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Watch upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria, especially about the foothills. Snow level lowering to 600 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 700 m where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Note, snow is expected fall as heavy rain at lower levels about the Canterbury foothills south of Methven and a separate Heavy Rain Watch is in force for the area. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected down to 900 metres at first and lowering to 600 metres from 10pm. Accumulations of 2 to 3 cm are possible about the Arthur's Pass summit and lesser amounts down to 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected from late tonight. Accumulations of 1 to 2 cm are possible above 800 metres and lesser amounts down to 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers falling to 500 metres. Accumulations of 3 to 5 cm are possible above 700 metres and lesser amounts down to 500 metres with most accumulating this morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected down to 900 metres at first and lowering to 600 metres from 10pm. 8 to 12 cm could accumulate about the summit with lesser amounts down to 600 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
